pi_items.source: Explicitly defined in setup_task.sh analysis brief header.
install_panoply.sh downloads NCEP Reanalysis files to this directory and setup_task.sh verifies their presence.
setup_task.sh creates this directory and export_result.sh checks for outputs here.
setup_task.sh and export_result.sh verify the existence of these specific output filenames.
setup_task.sh analysis brief specifically requests the February (Time index 1) baseline.
Python validation via xarray on slp.mon.ltm.nc confirms the Siberian High (global maximum in Feb) is ~1038 hPa.
Python validation via xarray on air.mon.ltm.nc confirms the minimum air temperature over the northern Sea of Okhotsk in Feb is ~-30 to -34 C.
Equivalent to Celsius range and accepted by the verifier.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real NCEP Reanalysis derived monthly long-term mean datasets (air.mon.ltm.nc and slp.mon.ltm.nc) downloaded directly from NOAA PSL. The required outputs must be placed in /home/ga/Documents/Ryuhyo. The expected minimum air temperature over the northern Sea of Okhotsk in February is approximately -31 to -34 °C, which correctly falls within the verifier's acceptable range of [-40, -5] °C (or [233, 268] K). The expected central pressure of the Siberian High is approximately 1038 hPa, fully within the expected [1025, 1045] hPa range.
